name: Ivanti Connect Secure zero-day mass exploitation
vendor_or_product: Ivanti Connect Secure / Policy Secure SSL-VPN and NAC gateway
vector: Chained authentication bypass plus command injection giving unauthenticated remote code execution on internet-facing gateway
impact_summary: Suspected Chinese state actor (UTA0178 / UNC5221) deployed custom webshells and backdoors for credential theft and lateral movement; CISA Emergency Directive ED 24-01 forced US federal disconnection
impact_quantified: Volexity found evidence of compromise on over 1700 ICS appliances

name: Fortinet FortiOS / FortiGate SSL-VPN mass exploitation
vendor_or_product: Fortinet FortiOS on FortiGate firewalls (SSL-VPN daemon) and FortiProxy
vector: Multi-year exploitation of critical SSL-VPN flaws (heap overflow / out-of-bounds write / auth bypass) as zero-day and n-day for unauthenticated RCE and super-admin access
impact_summary: Confirmed nation-state espionage: Chinese state actors compromised a FortiGate on the Netherlands Ministry of Defence network deploying the COATHANGER implant (per Dutch MIVD/AIVD); April 2025 CISA/Fortinet warned of symlink post-exploitation persistence surviving patching
impact_quantified: Earlier related CVE-2018-13379 leaked VPN credentials for approximately 50000 devices; the three headline SSL-VPN CVEs have no single corroborated victim-count aggregate

name: Cisco ArcaneDoor (ASA/FTD) and IOS XE Web UI mass implant
vendor_or_product: Cisco ASA / Firepower Threat Defense and Cisco IOS XE (routers and switches)
vector: State-sponsored ArcaneDoor espionage implants on ASA/FTD (UAT4356 / STORM-1849) plus mass privilege-escalation and command-injection compromise of IOS XE Web UI creating rogue admin accounts
impact_summary: Full device takeover of perimeter and backbone routing/switching infrastructure; ArcaneDoor assessed as government-focused espionage; IOS XE compromise was one of the largest network-infrastructure mass-compromise events
impact_quantified: Cortex Xpanse telemetry counted 22074 implanted IOS XE devices on 18 October 2023

name: Palo Alto PAN-OS GlobalProtect zero-day (Operation MidnightEclipse)
vendor_or_product: Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS firewalls with GlobalProtect gateway/portal
vector: Command injection in GlobalProtect giving unauthenticated arbitrary code execution as root (CVSS 10.0)
impact_summary: Actor UTA0218 (Volexity) deployed the UPSTYLE Python backdoor and reverse-proxy tooling, exfiltrated firewall configuration and moved laterally; NCSC published a dedicated advisory. A Volt Typhoon / Chinese-APT link was raised only as a low-confidence hypothesis, the confirmed actor label is UTA0218 and NOT confirmed as Volt Typhoon

name: MOVEit Transfer mass exploitation (Cl0p)
vendor_or_product: Progress MOVEit Transfer managed file-transfer software
vector: Cl0p (TA505 / FIN11) exploited an SQL-injection zero-day for unauthenticated RCE and database access, deploying the LEMURLOOT webshell for pure data-theft extortion
impact_summary: UK impact came through payroll provider Zellis, cascading to BBC, British Airways and Boots employee payroll data (names, National Insurance numbers, some bank details)
impact_quantified: 2700+ organisations and roughly 93 to 95 million individuals affected (Emsisoft tally); defensible financial range approximately 6.5 to 10 billion USD (Emsisoft/IBM 165 USD-per-record). The 12bn USD figure is NOT corroborated by an authoritative source

name: XZ Utils backdoor
vendor_or_product: XZ Utils / liblzma compression library (target: OpenSSH sshd via systemd)
vector: Multi-year social-engineering supply-chain compromise: a malicious maintainer (Jia Tan) planted an obfuscated backdoor in release tarballs (5.6.0 / 5.6.1) hooking the sshd RSA routine for pre-auth RCE (CVSS 10.0)
impact_summary: A near-miss not a mass compromise: backdoored versions reached only rolling/development branches (Fedora 40 beta/Rawhide, Debian unstable/testing, Kali, openSUSE Tumbleweed, Arch) and were caught by Andres Freund before propagating to stable enterprise releases

name: CUPS Linux printing vulnerabilities
vendor_or_product: OpenPrinting CUPS (cups-browsed, libcupsfilters, libppd, cups-filters) on Linux/Unix
vector: Chained flaws let an unauthenticated attacker register a malicious printer whose unvalidated IPP attributes yield arbitrary command execution via foomatic-rip on a triggered print job
impact_summary: Real-world RCE severity moderated because cups-browsed is not enabled by default on many distributions (Red Hat rated components Important not Critical); the verified high-impact angle is DDoS amplification
impact_quantified: Akamai showed CVE-2024-47176 enables approximately 600x DDoS amplification; found approximately 198000 internet-exposed devices of which approximately 58000 (34%) usable for reflection

name: Aisuru (TurboMirai) IoT DDoS botnet
vendor_or_product: Consumer/SOHO broadband routers, IP/CCTV cameras and DVRs running commodity OEM firmware
vector: Compromise of poorly secured consumer IoT via default/weak credentials plus known firmware exploits (Mirai-derived); no single defining CVE, aggregates many device-specific flaws; firmware-update-server propagation is researcher-reported (moderate confidence)
impact_summary: Record-breaking volumetric DDoS-for-hire against gaming platforms and internet infrastructure; UK-based compromised home routers/cameras become unwitting attack sources, relevant to the PSTI product-security regime
impact_quantified: Cloudflare mitigated a 29.7 Tbps (approximately 14.1 billion packets/second) Aisuru attack in Q3 2025; estimated 1 to 4 million infected hosts globally (vendor telemetry, treat as indicative)

name: KV-botnet / Volt Typhoon SOHO-router takedown
vendor_or_product: End-of-life SOHO routers (Cisco RV320/RV325, Netgear ProSafe)
vector: Exploitation of end-of-life unpatched SOHO routers no longer receiving vendor updates to build covert proxy infrastructure for PRC actor Volt Typhoon; no single headline CVE (unfixable EoL-hardware flaws)
impact_summary: FBI/DOJ obtained a court order (announced 31 January 2024) and remotely deleted KV-botnet malware and severed C2; removal was temporary (reboot could re-expose). Volt Typhoon assessed as pre-positioning for disruptive attacks on US CNI. UK NCSC co-sealed advisory AA24-038a and reporting confirms UK EoL Cisco routers were targeted

name: Hikvision IP camera mass compromise (Moobot/Mirai)
vendor_or_product: Hikvision IP camera and NVR product lines running vulnerable web-server firmware
vector: Unauthenticated pre-auth command injection in the embedded web server yielding root RCE with no credentials; compounded by weak default passwords; Moobot (Mirai variant) recruited devices into DDoS swarms
impact_summary: Full device takeover enabling surveillance-feed interception, lateral movement and DDoS conscription; APT41/APT10 interest is reported not established (moderate confidence). In November 2022 the UK Government ordered removal of Hikvision cameras from sensitive central-government sites
impact_quantified: As of 2023 approximately 80000 cameras remained exploitable (from a 285000-device sample) across approximately 2300 organisations in 100+ countries; UK had over 2000 vulnerable internet-facing endpoints

name: Unitronics PLC attacks on US water utilities (CyberAv3ngers)
vendor_or_product: Unitronics Vision-series PLCs with integrated HMI (water/wastewater sector)
vector: No software CVE: exploitation of internet-exposed OT devices protected only by the Unitronics factory default password 1111 on default TCP port 2101 (default/weak-credential)
impact_summary: IRGC-affiliated CyberAv3ngers defaced HMIs; at Aliquippa PA a water booster station was knocked offline and operators reverted to manual operation; no water-quality or public-safety impact and no access to the treatment plant itself

name: Volt Typhoon critical-infrastructure pre-positioning
vendor_or_product: No single product (IT/OT boundary; valid-account and Active Directory abuse; compromised EoL edge devices)
vector: Living-off-the-land (LOTL) abuse of legitimate built-in tools and valid accounts plus compromise of EoL SOHO routers for covert proxy infrastructure; no single defining CVE
impact_summary: PRC actor pre-positioned within US CNI IT/OT (communications, energy, transportation, water); agencies observed persistence for at least five years and 300+ days in a US electric utility OT network. UK NCSC is a named co-author of AA24-038A and warned the same techniques could hit UK CNI

name: FrostyGoop / BUSTLEBERM district-heating attack (Lviv)
vendor_or_product: ENCO heating controllers via Modbus TCP; initial access via a MikroTik router
vector: Go-based Windows malware speaking Modbus TCP over port 502 directly to controllers, manipulating ENCO holding registers so cold water circulated; initial access via an undetermined vulnerability in an internet-exposed MikroTik router (no CVE assigned, genuinely unidentified)
impact_summary: First public case of malware manipulating OT directly over Modbus for physical effect; Dragos noted Moscow-based IPs but did not formally attribute to a nation-state (unattributed but Russia-linked). Pattern maps to UK district-heating and utility OT
impact_quantified: Approximately 600 apartment buildings in the Sykhiv district lost heating for roughly 48 hours in sub-zero conditions (100000+ people affected across the district)

name: ESXiArgs ransomware (hypervisor layer)
vendor_or_product: VMware ESXi bare-metal (Type-1) hypervisor with OpenSLP enabled and internet-reachable
vector: Automated ransomware exploiting exposure of the OpenSLP service on TCP/UDP port 427; safest characterisation is OpenSLP-exposed unpatched/EoL ESXi (not every victim clearly matched CVE-2021-21974)
impact_summary: Second wave used a modified routine encrypting more of large files; each compromised host could take down dozens of VMs. UK-relevant by class (NHS trusts, local authorities and enterprises run heavily virtualised ESXi estates)
impact_quantified: CISA estimated more than 3800 servers compromised globally; Rapid7 found approximately 18500 internet-facing ESXi servers vulnerable; ransom demands approximately 2 BTC typical

name: LogoFAIL and BlackLotus UEFI firmware compromise
vendor_or_product: UEFI/BIOS firmware (AMI, Insyde, Phoenix IBVs) and the Windows boot manager
vector: LogoFAIL: image-parser heap-overflow/OOB flaws let a malicious boot-logo image achieve code execution below the OS before Secure Boot (Bootkitty exploits CVE-2023-40238). BlackLotus: exploits CVE-2022-21894 (Baton Drop) to bypass Secure Boot via a bring-your-own-vulnerable-binary downgrade
impact_summary: Firmware-level persistence surviving OS reinstall and disk replacement, bypassing Secure Boot, Intel Boot Guard and endpoint defences; the hardest compromise to remediate. Binarly reported hundreds of Intel/Lenovo/Acer models affected. UK Windows/Intel/Lenovo/Acer estate exposed pending firmware updates and DBX revocations

name: Log4Shell mass server/endpoint exploitation
vendor_or_product: Apache Log4j (ubiquitous Java logging library) embedded in servers, appliances and OT products
vector: JNDI message-lookup substitution: a crafted logged string makes Log4j fetch and execute attacker-controlled code from a remote endpoint; unauthenticated, remote, trivial (CVSS 10.0)
impact_summary: Exploited to deploy cryptominers, botnets, ransomware and for espionage/initial access; CISA CSRB called it an endemic vulnerability expected to be exploited for a decade. NCSC-UK confirmed UK exploitation scanning and co-authored the joint mitigation advisory. Disclosed December 2021 but mass-exploited through 2022
